text.title: Trade-off Stakeholder Communication Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every stakeholder-tailored trade-off briefing must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The same one-sentence "key risk" paragraph MUST appear verbatim in all four artefacts (exec, PM, engineer, ops). If any artefact softens or omits the risk, the bundle is invalid.

rationale.source: Internal — post-mortem audit of 14 architecture decisions

rule.rationale: Stakeholder surprise is the most expensive failure mode; identical risk wording across audiences makes the surprise impossible.

rule.statement: Exec-summary MUST be ≤120 words AND state both the gain and the survivable downside in the first 60 words. Decoration before substance is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Executives skim; if the trade-off is not in the lede, the trade-off is not communicated.

rule.statement: Each artefact MUST address its role's primary concern: exec → cost/risk-to-business, PM → roadmap/dependency impact, engineer → option mechanics + what was sacrificed, ops → runbook/oncall delta. A briefing that uses identical body text for all four roles is invalid.
rule.rationale: Different stakeholders make different decisions with the same data; the artefact must surface the decision-relevant fields.

rule.statement: The engineer-note MUST list what we sacrificed by choosing this option (≥1 explicit "we gave up X" line). ADRs that document only the chosen option without the sacrifice are invalid.
rule.rationale: Future architects assume the current implementation is optimal; without the documented sacrifice they add complexity on top instead of understanding the trade.

rule.statement: A separate reviewer (subagent or human) MUST verify the four artefacts agree on the risk paragraph BEFORE the bundle is approved. Single-author no-review bundles are forbidden for Type-1 decisions.

rationale.source: Internal — close-rate audit on ADR briefings 2025

rule.rationale: The author of the technical analysis is not a reliable editor of their own summary; a separate review pass catches the laundered risk.
